accepted_text: Accepted extensions depend on the selected language and the active project profile.
admin_filter: Filter last name, first name, status, group, heuristic…
admin_help: Raw submission view. This page is protected by an administrator session.
admin_summary_help: Pedagogical and technical summary of submissions.
already_active: A submission is already queued or running for this token.
archive_generation_linux: Go to the submission directory, then click make_submission_linux.sh.
archive_generation_windows: Go to the submission directory, then click make_submission_windows.bat.
browser_fingerprint_help: Browser fingerprint generated automatically to limit impersonation.
cancel_confirm: Confirm cancellation or withdrawal of this submission?
cancel_not_allowed: This submission cannot be canceled from this browser.
cancel_requested_msg: Cancellation requested. Stopping the running container if needed.
client_mismatch: This token is already bound to another workstation or browser. Contact the instructor if this is wrong.
compilation_text: the build command depends on the selected language profile.
compile_logs_help: These outputs help diagnose a server-side compilation error.
compile_resource_error_msg: Compilation error: the compiler was probably stopped by a Docker memory/resource limit.
confirm_purge: Confirm deletion of all submissions for this student?
confirm_purge_all: Confirm purging all submissions, whatever their status or token?
cooldown: You must wait before submitting again.
data_text: do not include project data files or instances in the ZIP.
heuristic_help: This value is stored locally in your browser.
home_lead: Upload your ZIP archive. The server builds and evaluates your program in an isolated container according to the active project.
interrupted_msg: Evaluation interrupted by a server shutdown. Submit again if needed.
leaderboard_help: Best completed non-canceled submission per student. Automatic refresh.
legend_help: Q = queued, RUN = running, OK = done or valid instance, CANCELED = canceled/withdrawn submission.
local_submissions_help: Submissions known by this browser. Click a row to open the details.
missing_expected_file_msg: Expected file missing: no main.cpp found in the ZIP archive.
no_local_submissions: No submission is known in this browser.
output_format_intro: Your program must write the output format expected by the active project to stdout for each instance.
output_truncated: Output truncated to the configured limit.
privacy_access_text: Students can access only the upload page, their own local browser information and public leaderboards. The teacher, through the administration page protected by an admin key, can view uploads, results, technical logs, leaderboards and incident states, and can cancel, purge, pause or resume uploads. Files and results are stored under the local MAAT server directories projects/<project_id>/documents/, projects/<project_id>/results/, submissions/ and runs/.
privacy_data_text: The server processes the information required for the lab session: group, first name, last name, symbol, individual token, heuristic name, uploaded ZIP archive, compilation/execution logs, per-instance results and timestamps. The token is used only to identify a student during the session.
privacy_lead: This page describes the technical handling of data used during the MAAT session.
privacy_limits_text: A token coupon must remain confidential: anyone who photographs or copies it can try to impersonate the corresponding student. Students must not share their coupon. If a mistake or impersonation is suspected, the teacher can intervene from the administration page.
privacy_security_text: MAAT limits ZIP archive size and content, rejects unsafe paths and symbolic links, runs compilation and programs inside Docker containers with time, memory, CPU and process limits, enforces a minimum delay between uploads, can bind a token to the first workstation/browser used, masks the token input field, encrypts exchanges through HTTPS/TLS when the tunnel is used, and lets the teacher pause new uploads.
privacy_tunnel_text: When the Cloudflare tunnel is enabled, browser connections to the public URL are encrypted with HTTPS/TLS up to Cloudflare, then routed through the encrypted Cloudflare tunnel to the local MAAT server started by the teacher. The tunnel exposes the server without directly opening the lab network. Uploaded data therefore travels in encrypted form before reaching the local server.
rules_cooldown: One accepted submission every {minutes} minutes per student.
rules_one_active: Only one queued or running submission per token.
rules_ranking: The ranking keeps the best completed non-canceled submission.
rules_run_parallel: Parallel execution: up to {threads} thread(s), Docker quota {cpus} CPU.
rules_run_time: Maximum execution time per instance: {value} s.
rules_security: The token is bound to the first workstation/browser that uses it.
session_finished: The lab session time is over: new submissions are disabled.
submissions_paused: New submissions are temporarily paused by the instructor.
token_help: The token is stored locally in your browser.
zip_format_intro: The archive must contain your C++ sources and a discoverable main.cpp file.
